exam.name: National Council Licensure Examination for Practical Nurses (NCLEX-PN)
exam.category_description: Exam-accurate nursing licensure prep built from the official NCSBN test plan, organized into clear, retention-focused study topics.
exam.hero_subheadline: Study every tested client-needs domain in 15-minute topics mapped directly to the official NCSBN blueprint.
exam.companion_problem: Most NCLEX prep feels scattered, so learners waste time on content that is not prioritized by the actual exam blueprint.
exam.companion_solution: We convert the official test plan into a complete, structured curriculum with concise notes designed for long-term retention and exam readiness.

sections.title: Safe and Effective Care Environment: Coordinated Care
sections.description: Collaborate with health care team members to facilitate effective client care, covering advocacy, delegation, client rights, and ethical practice.

topics.title: Advance Directives and Advocacy
topics.description: Provide information about advance directives, review client understanding, and advocate for client rights and needs.
learning_objectives: Review client understanding of advance directives, such as a living will or health care proxy.
Discuss identified treatment options with the client and respect the decisions made.
Promote client self-advocacy and perform care to support unbiased treatment.

topics.title: Assignments and Delegation
topics.description: Assign client care and related tasks to assistive personnel or LPN/VNs effectively.
learning_objectives: Compare the needs of the client to the knowledge, skills, and abilities of assistive personnel prior to making assignments.
Provide information to supervisors when client care assignments need to be changed.
Utilize the rights of delegation (e.g., right task, right circumstances, right person, right direction/communication, right supervision/evaluation).

topics.title: Client Rights and Informed Consent
topics.description: Understand client rights, the informed consent process, and individual confidentiality.
learning_objectives: Inform the client of individual rights, including confidentiality and informed consent.
Identify the appropriate person to provide informed consent for the client.
Describe the components of informed consent such as the purpose and risks of the procedure.

topics.title: Collaboration and Management Concepts
topics.description: Collaborate with the interdisciplinary team and apply concepts of management and supervision.
learning_objectives: Identify the roles and responsibilities of health care team members.
Contribute to the development and update of the client plan of care.
Recognize and report staff conflict and verify abilities of staff members to perform assigned tasks.

topics.title: Continuity of Care and Establishing Priorities
topics.description: Follow up with clients after discharge, manage care transitions, and organize care based on client needs.
learning_objectives: Participate in client discharge, transfer, and follow up on unresolved issues.
Record client information securely and provide/receive shift reports.
Organize and prioritize care based on client needs and use effective time management skills.

topics.title: Legal Rights, Ethical Practice, and Quality Improvement
topics.description: Practice according to the code of ethics, understand legal responsibilities, and participate in quality improvement.
learning_objectives: Identify ethical issues affecting staff or clients and intervene to promote ethical practice.
Follow regulations and policies for reporting specific issues (e.g., abuse, neglect, communicable diseases).
Identify the impact of performance and quality improvement activities on client care outcomes.

sections.title: Safe and Effective Care Environment: Safety and Infection Prevention and Control
sections.description: Contribute to the protection of clients and health care personnel from health and environmental hazards.

topics.title: Accident and Injury Prevention
topics.description: Identify factors influencing accident prevention, verify client identity, and use transfer assistive devices safely.
learning_objectives: Identify client allergies and intervene appropriately.
Determine client and staff member knowledge of safety procedures.
Use facility client identification procedures and monitor the care environment for safety hazards.

topics.title: Emergency Response Plan and Hazardous Materials
topics.description: Prepare for disaster responses and handle hazardous and infectious materials safely.
learning_objectives: Identify nursing and assistive personnel roles during internal and external disasters.
Demonstrate knowledge of facility protocols for handling hazardous and infectious materials.
Identify and employ methods to control the spread of infectious agents.

topics.title: Restraints, Safety Devices, and Safe Use of Equipment
topics.description: Apply least restrictive restraints properly, document client response, and follow safe equipment protocols.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate knowledge of the appropriate application of restraints and safety devices.
Implement least restrictive restraints or seclusion and document usage.
Ensure availability and safe functioning of client care equipment according to facility protocols.

topics.title: Standard and Transmission-Based Precautions
topics.description: Apply principles of infection control, asepsis, and standard/transmission-based precautions.
learning_objectives: Identify communicable diseases and modes of transmission (e.g., airborne, droplet, contact).
Apply principles of infection control including personal protective equipment (PPE) and aseptic technique.
Set up a sterile field and prevent environmental spread of infectious disease.

sections.title: Health Promotion and Maintenance
sections.description: Provide nursing care that incorporates the expected stages of growth and development and the prevention and early detection of health problems.

topics.title: Ante/Intra/Postpartum and Newborn Care
topics.description: Assist with care during the antepartum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods, as well as newborn care.
learning_objectives: Assist with monitoring a client in labor and monitoring the recovery of a stable postpartum client.
Identify client emotional preparedness for pregnancy and assist in performing nonstress tests.
Reinforce client teaching on infant care skills such as feeding, bathing, and positioning.

topics.title: Developmental Stages, Transitions, and Aging
topics.description: Compare clients to developmental milestones and identify expected changes related to aging.
learning_objectives: Identify client knowledge of the aging process and reinforce teaching on expected changes.
Identify and report client deviations from expected growth and development.
Assist the client with expected life transitions such as attachment to newborn, parenting, and retirement.

topics.title: Health Promotion, Disease Prevention, and Data Collection
topics.description: Perform baseline data collection, identify risk factors, and promote healthy lifestyle choices.
learning_objectives: Collect data for health history, including client medical and family medical history.
Identify clients in need of immunizations and recognize unexpected responses to immunizations.
Check results of client health screening tests and provide information for prevention of high-risk behaviors.

sections.title: Psychosocial Integrity
sections.description: Provide care that assists with the promotion and support of the emotional, mental, and social well-being of clients.

topics.title: Abuse, Neglect, and Crisis Intervention
topics.description: Recognize and intervene in cases of abuse, neglect, and behavioral crises.
learning_objectives: Identify signs and symptoms of physical, psychological, or financial abuse or neglect in clients.
Identify clients in crisis and determine their risk for self-injury or violence.
Assist in managing the care of an angry or agitated client using de-escalation techniques.

topics.title: Mental Health Concepts and Coping Mechanisms
topics.description: Assess psychosocial functioning, mental health conditions, and client coping mechanisms.
learning_objectives: Identify client symptoms of acute or chronic mental illness such as schizophrenia, depression, or bipolar disorder.
Recognize client use of effective and ineffective defense and coping mechanisms.
Monitor client appearance, mood, and psychomotor behavior and observe for changes.

topics.title: Cultural/Spiritual Influences and End-of-Life Care
topics.description: Incorporate cultural and spiritual awareness into care and provide support during grief and end-of-life.
learning_objectives: Identify the importance of client self-reported culture/ethnicity when planning and providing care.
Recognize the effect of a client's self-reported religious/spiritual beliefs on the plan of care.
Identify client end-of-life needs and support the client in anticipatory grieving.

topics.title: Substance Use and Therapeutic Communication
topics.description: Manage care for substance use disorders and use therapeutic communication effectively.
learning_objectives: Identify signs and symptoms of substance misuse, withdrawal, and overdose.
Plan and provide care to clients experiencing substance-related withdrawal or toxicity.
Use therapeutic communication techniques to establish a trusting nurse-client relationship.

sections.title: Physiological Integrity: Basic Care and Comfort
sections.description: Provide comfort to clients and assistance in the performance of activities of daily living.

topics.title: Mobility, Immobility, and Assistive Devices
topics.description: Promote mobility, prevent complications of immobility, and use assistive devices properly.
learning_objectives: Check client for mobility, gait, strength, and motor skills.
Provide for mobility needs including ambulation, range of motion, transfer, and repositioning.
Identify appropriate use of assistive devices such as canes, walkers, and crutches.

topics.title: Nutrition, Hydration, and Elimination
topics.description: Ensure proper nutritional intake, hydration, and manage bowel and bladder elimination protocols.
learning_objectives: Check client feeding tube placement and patency, and provide feeding for clients with enteral tubes.
Monitor and provide for the nutritional needs of the client, identifying potential for aspiration.
Provide care to the client with bowel or bladder management protocols and perform irrigations.

topics.title: Nonpharmacological Comfort, Hygiene, and Rest
topics.description: Administer nonpharmacological comfort measures, support personal hygiene, and promote rest.
learning_objectives: Evaluate pain using standardized rating scales and provide nonpharmacological measures for pain relief.
Assist with activities of daily living and determine client usual personal hygiene habits.
Identify client rest and sleep patterns and provide measures to promote adequate sleep.

sections.title: Physiological Integrity: Pharmacological Therapies
sections.description: Provide care related to the administration of medications and monitor clients who are receiving parenteral therapies.

topics.title: Medication Administration and Dosage Calculations
topics.description: Follow the rights of medication administration and accurately perform dosage calculations.
learning_objectives: Perform calculations needed for medication administration using clinical decision-making.
Follow the rights of medication administration and maintain medication safety practices.
Administer medications by oral, subcutaneous, intramuscular, enteral, and topical routes.

topics.title: Adverse Effects, Contraindications, and Interactions
topics.description: Monitor and document adverse effects, allergic reactions, and interactions of pharmacological agents.
learning_objectives: Identify a contraindication to the administration of a prescribed or over-the-counter medication.
Identify symptoms of an allergic reaction and implement procedures to counteract adverse effects.
Monitor anticipated interactions among client prescribed medications, fluids, and herbal supplements.

topics.title: Expected Actions and Pharmacological Pain Management
topics.description: Understand the intended actions of drugs and monitor pain control devices.
learning_objectives: Identify client expected responses to medication and apply knowledge of pathophysiology.
Evaluate client response to medication, including therapeutic effects and critical laboratory values.
Maintain pain control devices, such as epidurals and patient-controlled analgesia, and monitor response.

sections.title: Physiological Integrity: Reduction of Risk Potential
sections.description: Reduce the potential for clients to develop complications or health problems related to treatments, procedures, or existing conditions.

topics.title: Vital Signs and Laboratory Values
topics.description: Monitor, compare, and report client vital signs and laboratory test results.
learning_objectives: Monitor and evaluate client vital signs, comparing them to baseline information.
Compare client laboratory values to normal laboratory values.
Perform venipuncture for blood draws and monitor diagnostic or laboratory test results.

topics.title: Diagnostic Tests and Therapeutic Procedures
topics.description: Assist with and perform diagnostic tests and therapeutic procedures while monitoring for complications.
learning_objectives: Perform electrocardiograms (EKG/ECG) and point-of-care testing such as blood glucose monitoring.
Insert, maintain, and remove nasogastric tubes and urinary catheters.
Maintain central venous catheters and maintain/remove peripheral intravenous catheters.

topics.title: Potential for Alterations and Surgical Complications
topics.description: Identify risks for complications in body systems and prevent adverse outcomes from surgical procedures.
learning_objectives: Identify signs or symptoms of potential prenatal complications.
Recognize changes in client neurologic status and perform focused data collection.
Assist with care before and after surgical procedures and monitor client responses.

sections.title: Physiological Integrity: Physiological Adaptation
sections.description: Participate in providing care for clients with acute, chronic, or life-threatening physical health conditions.

topics.title: Alterations in Body Systems and Pathophysiology
topics.description: Recognize signs of acute and chronic illness and provide interventions based on pathophysiology.
learning_objectives: Identify signs and symptoms of infection and basic abnormalities on a cardiac monitor strip.
Provide care for a client with a tracheostomy, ostomy, or on a ventilator.
Apply knowledge of pathophysiology to monitoring clients for alterations in body systems.

topics.title: Fluid and Electrolyte Imbalances
topics.description: Identify and manage fluid and electrolyte imbalances in clients.
learning_objectives: Provide care for a client with a fluid and electrolyte imbalance.
Identify signs and symptoms of client fluid and/or electrolyte imbalances.
Monitor client response to interventions to correct fluid and/or electrolyte imbalances.

topics.title: Medical Emergencies and Unexpected Responses
topics.description: Respond appropriately to life-threatening emergencies and unexpected negative responses to therapy.
learning_objectives: Respond and intervene to a client life-threatening situation, such as cardiopulmonary resuscitation.
Provide emergency care for wound disruption, such as evisceration or dehiscence.
Identify and treat a client experiencing an intravenous line infiltration or an unexpected negative response to therapy.
